name: recall_stig_xlsx_rhel_maps_cm6
description: RECALL case — expected to FAIL pre-RAG-rewrite. One of the two real STIG .xlsx files in the BoE that tagged to ZERO. This is a STIG checklist EXPORTED TO XLSX (not a .ckl/.cklb), so it arrives as kind=xlsx with NO structured stig_findings list — just spreadsheet-extracted text (rule titles, status column). The hardening rules map to CM-6 (Configuration Settings). The failure mode: the tagger's inline CCI scrape (Tier 2) is gated to _STRUCTURED_FINDING_KINDS, which XLSX is NOT, so even though a 'CCI-000366' token literally appears in the extracted text it is never scraped; Tier 3 _CONTROL_ID_RE only matches uppercase IDs and there is no 'CM-6' string in the body; Tier 4 needs an evidence_type that wasn't classified. Result: zero tags. Distractor SI-2 present as precision guard.

objectives.text: The organization establishes and documents configuration settings for information technology products employed within the information system using security configuration checklists that reflect the most restrictive mode consistent with operational requirements.
objectives.implementation_guidance: Apply a DISA STIG hardening baseline and remediate open findings.

objectives.text: The organization installs security-relevant software updates within the organization-defined time period of the release of the updates.
objectives.implementation_guidance: Apply patches within the policy window.

evidence.title: RHEL 8 STIG results export
evidence.text:
Vuln ID	Rule Title	Status	CCI	Severity
V-230221	RHEL 8 must enable the SELinux targeted policy	NotAFinding	CCI-000366	medium
V-230264	RHEL 8 must disable kernel core dumps	NotAFinding	CCI-000366	low
V-230311	RHEL 8 must set the umask to 077 for interactive users	NotAFinding	CCI-000366	medium
V-230502	RHEL 8 file permissions must be configured to protect log files	Open	CCI-000366	medium
